extra.message: User-controlled data from post_id is concatenated directly into a SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(). This allows SQL injection via string manipulation even through the weak regex filter.

Multiple examples of SQL injection via string concatenation, .format(), % formatting, and f-strings with user input directly embedded in SQL query strings. Each line builds SQL by embedding unsanitized user input.

User-controlled self.path is appended to the working directory without sufficient sanitization, then passed directly to open(). The regex only blocks /../../ but not single-level ../, URL-encoded variants (%2e%2e%2f), absolute paths (/etc/passwd), or other traversal patterns. This allows arbitrary file read (LFI).

User-controlled URL from request.args is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen() with only a weak prefix check. The check 'url.startswith("https://insecurewebapp.com")' can be bypassed using subdomain techniques (insecurewebapp.com.attacker.com), credentials (insecurewebapp.com@attacker.com), or path tricks. This allows SSRF to internal networks and arbitrary hosts.

The username variable (decrypted from user-supplied session cookie) is interpolated directly into HTML output via string formatting with no output encoding or escaping. If an attacker can control the encrypted cookie value, this leads to stored/reflected XSS.

The username variable is interpolated directly into HTML output via string formatting with no output encoding. User-controlled data written unsanitized into HTTP response body enables Cross-Site Scripting.

User-controlled search_query from request.args.get('q') and comments stored via POST are passed to the Jinja2 template. If autoescape were disabled or the template used |safe filter, this would permit reflected and stored XSS. The template index.html at line 30 renders search_query unsafely into HTML attribute context.

Username.pickle() is called on user-supplied cookie data. The comments explicitly state 'Calls pickle.loads on user supplied info' and 'No signature present or anything preventing the sending of a malicious pickle object.' Pickle deserialization of untrusted data leads to arbitrary code execution.

AES-CBC decryption is performed without any authentication tag (HMAC/GCM). The encrypted cookie is decrypted using unpad(aes.decrypt(encd)). This is vulnerable to padding oracle attacks where an attacker can modify the ciphertext and observe server response (500 vs 200) to decrypt arbitrary data or forge valid sessions.

cwe: CWE-649: Reliance on Obfuscation or Encryption
CWE-354: Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value

extra.message: Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'qwerty12345'. A weak, hardcoded secret key allows attackers to forge session cookies, CSRF tokens, and other signed data. The key is also trivially guessable (a common weak password).
Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as '55555'. A weak, hardcoded secret key allows attackers to forge session cookies, CSRF tokens, and other signed data.

Payment signatures are computed using SHA256(secret + message) instead of HMAC. SHA256 is vulnerable to length extension attacks, allowing an attacker to append data to the payment_info and compute a valid signature without knowing the secret. This can modify transaction amounts.

cwe: CWE-354: Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value

extra.message: Payment signatures are computed using SHA256(secret + message) instead of HMAC. SHA256 is vulnerable to length extension attacks, allowing an attacker to append data to the payment_info and compute a valid signature without knowing the secret.

cwe: CWE-354: Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value

extra.message: SSL context check_hostname is explicitly set to False, disabling TLS hostname verification. This makes the connection vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks since the certificate hostname is never validated against the actual server hostname.

A malicious PickleExploit class uses __reduce__ to execute subprocess.Popen with attacker-controlled commands. When combined with pickle deserialization, this allows arbitrary OS command execution. The payload is base64+compress encoded and sent to the target server.

Exploit class RCE uses __reduce__ to execute os.system with an attacker-specified reverse shell command. When a server deserializes this pickle payload via pickle.loads(), it achieves remote code execution.

HTTPServer binds to 0.0.0.0, exposing the vulnerable path traversal application to all network interfaces including external networks. Combined with the path traversal vulnerability, this exposes local file read to network attackers.
HTTPServer binds to 0.0.0.0, exposing the XSS-vulnerable and CBC-padding-oracle-vulnerable application to all network interfaces.
